label: Conflict Resolution Priorities
description: Normalized priorities used when two evidence artifacts or statements disagree materially.
usageNotes: Apply in priority order until the conflict is resolved or escalated.
If priorities remain tied, route to governance instead of silently choosing.

entries.label: Source Priority
entries.description: Prefer higher evidence-level sources over lower-level sources.
usageNotes: Directive generally precedes synthesis; synthesis generally precedes single studies.

entries.label: Recency Priority
entries.description: Prefer the more current artifact when methodological rigor is not lower.
usageNotes: Do not let newer but weaker commentary overrule stronger mature evidence automatically.

entries.label: Certainty Priority
entries.description: Prefer the statement with higher certainty when source level is comparable.

entries.label: Directness Priority
entries.description: Prefer evidence that more directly matches target population, intervention and setting.
usageNotes: Critical for translational and indirect comparison evidence.

entries.label: Jurisdiction Priority
entries.description: Prefer sources valid for the target legal or governance jurisdiction.
usageNotes: Local and national sources may outrank global sources for execution legality.

entries.label: Implementation Priority
entries.description: Use implementation feasibility to downgrade readiness or trigger review, not to negate stronger clinical truth.
usageNotes: Operational infeasibility should create variance or adaptation records.
